projects.title: Unit 4 Project — Rates & Percents in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: solve real-world percent problems like discounts and tips.

projects.title: Discount Dash
projects.desc: Arcade game solving percent problems with discounts and sale prices (6.RP.3c).

projects.title: Graphic Novel — Shopping Mall Mogul
projects.desc: Story-driven adventure solving percent problems in a shopping mall.

title: Use Percent to Solve Problems
contentObjective: I can solve problems involving discounts, markups, tax, and tips using percents.
languageObjective: I can explain my solution using the words discount, markup, tax, tip, and percent.

turnAndTalk.question: At the prize shop a bear is 25% off but the shop adds 5% tax. Which of these raises the price and which lowers it, and what do the words discount, markup, tax, and tip have in common?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A discount lowers the price, while tax, tip, and markup raise it.

stems.en: A ___ makes the price go down, but a ___ makes it go up.
stems.es: Un ___ hace que el precio baje, pero un ___ hace que suba.

stems.en: They are alike because each is a ___ of the price.
stems.es: Se parecen porque cada uno es un ___ del precio.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students sort discount as lowering price and tax/tip/markup as raising it, and recognize all are a percent of a price.
turnAndTalk.extend: Does it matter whether the shop takes the discount first or adds the tax first? Predict, and explain your reasoning before you calculate.
extendStems: I think the order does / does not matter because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: In your table you found the discount, then the tax. Why do we calculate the tax on the price AFTER the discount instead of the original price?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Tax is charged on the lower price you actually pay after the discount.

stems.en: I find the discount first because ___.
Then I take 5% tax on the ___ price, which is ___.
stems.es: Luego tomo 5% de impuesto sobre el precio ___, que es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain the discount lowers the price first, then tax is applied to that new lower price (e.g., 5% of 72 = 3.6).
turnAndTalk.extend: A classmate added the 5% tax and the 25% discount into one '20% off' step. Explain why combining them like that gives the wrong final price.
extendStems: Tax and discount cannot just be added because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: An arcade membership is $50, marked 30% off, then 6% tax is added. Your friend says the total is $37.10. Is that right, and how would you prove it?
turnAndTalk.kernel: After 30% off the price is $35, then 6% tax makes the total $37.10.

stems.en: First the discount: 30% of $50 = $___, so the sale price is $___.
stems.es: Primero el descuento: 30% de $50 = $___, así que el precio rebajado es $___.

stems.en: Then 6% tax on $___ adds $___, for a total of $___.
stems.es: Luego 6% de impuesto sobre $___ suma $___, para un total de $___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute $15 discount, $35 sale price, $2.10 tax, and $37.10 total, confirming the friend is correct with shown steps.
turnAndTalk.extend: Suppose the membership were a tip situation instead: $50 plus a 20% tip. Compare that total to the $37.10 discount-then-tax total and explain why one is so much higher.
extendStems: A 20% tip makes the total $___ because ___.

vocabulary.definition: Money taken off the first price to make it cheaper.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dinero que se quita del precio original para que sea más barato.
vocabulary.cloze: An amount taken off the original price is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: Money added to the cost to set the selling price.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dinero que se suma al costo para fijar el precio de venta.
vocabulary.visual: A $30 item with 10% markup sells for $33
vocabulary.cloze: An amount added to a store's cost to set a higher selling price is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: Extra money added to a price for the government.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dinero extra que se suma a un precio para el gobierno.
vocabulary.cloze: An extra percent added to a purchase that goes to the government is ___.

vocabulary.definition: Extra money you give for good service.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dinero extra que das por un buen servicio.
vocabulary.cloze: Extra money added for good service, often a percent of the bill, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A way to compare a number to 100, shown with the % sign.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una manera de comparar un número con 100, con el signo %.
vocabulary.cloze: A number out of 100 is a ___.

launch.narrative: Welcome to the arcade prize shop! You've been saving up tickets and now it's time to spend them. Some prizes are on sale with discounts, but the shop also charges a 5% ticket tax on purchases. Plus, there's a 10% markup on limited-edition prizes. Can you figure out the final ticket cost for each prize?
launch.contextImage: Arcade prize counter with stuffed animals and electronics showing price tags with discount and tax labels
noticePrompts: What happens to the price when there is a discount?
What happens to the price when tax is added?
wonderPrompts: Does it matter whether you apply the discount or tax first?
How do you find the total cost after both a discount and a tax?

explore.instructions: Complete the table to find the final ticket cost for each arcade prize after discounts and taxes.

discourse.prompt: Why do we apply the discount before calculating the tax?
discourse.sentenceFrame: I find the discount first because ___, then I calculate tax on the ___ price, which is ___.

optional.stem: A test has 25 questions. A student gets 88% correct. How many are correct?
A shirt is $30 after a 25% discount off the original price. What was the original price?

approaching.stem: A prize costs 120 tickets and is 25% off. What is the sale price?
A meal costs $20. You leave a 15% tip. How much is the tip?
A shirt costs $40 and the sales tax is 5%. What is the total cost including tax?
A game originally costs 200 tickets. It is marked up 10%. What is the new price?

approaching.instructions: Sort each situation: does the final price go UP or DOWN?

rows: A $45 game has a 20% discount. What is the sale price?
A $60 controller has 8% tax. What is the total?
A $25 meal with 15% tip. What is the total?

onLevel.stem: A store marks up a $30 item by 40%, then a customer gets 10% off. What is the final price?

onLevel.instructions: A $80 pair of shoes is 25% off. Use the bar model to show the discount and sale price.
onLevel.label: 25% of $80 = $20 discount. You pay 75% = $60.

onLevel.stem: A restaurant bill is $35. You want to leave a 20% tip. What is the total amount you pay?

extending.title: Find the Discount Error

workedExample.label: A prize costs 160 tickets at 25% off. Find the sale price.

hints: The student forgot to convert the percent to a decimal before multiplying.

extending.prompt: A store has a 20% off sale, then takes an additional 10% off the sale price. Is this the same as 30% off the original? Use $100 as the original price. Show your work for both scenarios and explain the difference.
extending.sentenceFrame: Taking 20% off then 10% off gives a final price of $___, but 30% off the original gives $___. The difference is $___ because ___.

extending.instructions: You are shopping at the arcade prize store. Calculate the final price for each item after discount AND 6% tax.

connect.scenario: You want to buy an arcade membership that costs $50. The arcade is running a 30% off sale, and there is a 6% sales tax applied after the discount. Your friend says the total is $37.10.
connect.promptQuestion: Is your friend correct? Show your work to prove it.
connect.prompt: First I find the discount: 30% of $50 = $___. The sale price is $50 − $___ = $___. Then I add 6% tax: $___ × 0.06 = $___. The total is $___ + $___ = $___.

exitTicket.stem: A jacket costs $80 and is 35% off. Sales tax is 5%. What is the final price?
